findings.problem: The second scene proves the same point too many times in the same shape before it escalates. Mrs. Rusk, Bess, and Nora are all presented as sequential witness rooms where Inez brings Silas inside, a household worker names a practical cost, and Silas absorbs another rebuke. Because only the Nora/Ettie room turns the demonstration into immediate bodily consequence, the earlier two stops flatten into a procedural case-study march instead of a steadily tightening confrontation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the household-tour sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:141, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:179, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:217 so each stop changes the chapter's pressure rather than restating it. Compress or merge one of the first two rooms, or give the Bess passage beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:179 a new turning function that materially alters Silas's stance before the Nora/Ettie escalation beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:217.
findings.acceptance_test: Reading the revised passage from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:141 through the burn sequence beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:257, a reviewer must be able to identify a distinct dramatic function for each remaining stop on first read: one demonstrates unstable heat, one introduces a genuinely new category of consequence or forces a visible change in Silas's behavior, and the Nora/Ettie room escalates into immediate injury. If the middle can still be summarized as three consecutive versions of 'another household explains why bridelight is bad,' it fails.

findings.problem: The chapter leans too often on the same emphatic sentence formula: negation or restatement in clipped fragments that immediately redefine the thought ('Not a steady color, not yet'; 'Not an introduction offered for courtesy'; 'Not pain yet'; 'Not learning. Playing with it.'; 'Furious was easy. Furious was a mood.'). Used this many times in one chapter, the move stops feeling pressure-shaped and starts reading like an audible house tic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence architecture at the beats anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:39, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:51, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:203, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:283, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:325. Keep only the strongest one or two negation/redefinition beats and recast the rest into fresh syntax that derives emphasis from image, action, or cadence specific to the local moment rather than from the same fragmentary reversal.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised chapter, a reviewer reading the cited passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:39, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:51, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:203, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:283, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:325 should hear no more than two beats whose main emphasis comes from a sentence-initial negation or repeated-noun restatement. If three or more of those moments still rely on the same 'Not X / X was...' pattern, it fails.

summary: Chapter 02 cleanly lands its confrontation brief: Inez arrives with labor-earned authority, the moss responds to public naming in real time, household costs become bodily and local, continuity remains intact, and the chapter stays in close-third past tense. It fails review-stage craft and prose, though, because the middle household tour repeats its demonstrative beat before the burn lands, and the chapter leans too often on the same negation-redefinition sentence pattern, which flattens sentence-level pressure.
